title: The Twenty-sixth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## November 17, 2024 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

November 17, 2024 falls on the Twenty-sixth Sunday after Pentecost (also called Proper 28 in the Revised Common Lectionary), placing the church in Ordinary Time near the end of the liturgical year. The RCL readings for this Sunday typically include Judges 4:1-7 (or 1 Thessalonians 5:1-11), Psalm 123, and Matthew 25:14-30 (the Parable of the Talents), with the Gospel emphasizing stewardship and accountability before Christ's return. This Sunday falls just one week before Advent begins (December 1, 2024), making it part of the final Sundays of the church year with eschatological themes prominent.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a post-General Convention period, with the 81st General Convention having concluded in Louisville, Kentucky in June 2024, having addressed ongoing debates about sexuality, blessing of same-sex unions, and the future direction of the denomination. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kym Lucas (elected 2021), was navigating the pastoral complexities following General Convention decisions while managing local parish life. No major Denver-area Episcopal news dominated the immediate week of November 17, though the denomination remained internally focused on implementing Convention resolutions across dioceses.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early-to-mid November weather patterns with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit and occasional precipitation typical of late autumn in the Front Range. Mayor Mike Johnston's administration was actively addressing homelessness and public safety concerns in downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood where Saint John's Cathedral is located (1313 Clarkson Street). The Colorado state government was preparing for winter legislative sessions while processing results from the November 5 election cycle, where Colorado voters had passed several ballot measures including property tax and education funding initiatives.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President-elect Donald Trump was in the immediate post-election period following his November 5 victory and was announcing cabinet positions and transition team members, creating significant political uncertainty and media focus. The stock market was experiencing volatility amid economic policy speculation and Fed rate expectations, with inflation remaining a dominant concern for many American households. Gaza conflict coverage continued to dominate international news but also divided American public opinion, with ongoing protests and campus activism around Middle East policy.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Ukraine-Russia conflict remained active with winter warfare tactics beginning as temperatures dropped, and international attention focused on potential diplomatic shifts given Trump's stated interest in negotiating the conflict. North Korea had escalated military activities and rhetoric, including alleged troop deployments to support Russian forces in Ukraine, signaling broader geopolitical realignment concerns. The Middle East remained tense following months of Gaza conflict, with humanitarian crises dominating international discourse and shaping global diplomatic relationships.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was in a post-election processing phase marked by political division and anxiety about the coming transition of power, with religious communities navigating questions about faith, politics, and national direction. The holiday season was beginning to emerge in cultural messaging and retail spaces, creating a backdrop of consumer expectations and family-centered cultural pressure alongside political turbulence. The Denver Broncos were mid-season (4-5 record at this point in November 2024), and sports offered a familiar communal focal point for the city amid broader national tensions.
